root_cause.description: The v2.4.1 deployment introduced a cache invalidation that forced all requests to hit the database directly. A new unoptimized query (full table scan joining orders and products without proper indexing) caused database connections to saturate the 200-connection pool, leading to cascading request queuing and timeouts.

contributing_factors.factor: Missing database index on orders.created_at and orders.product_id columns
Full cache invalidation instead of selective invalidation
Database connection pool sized too small for the load pattern

causal_chain: Deployment of v2.4.1 triggered full cache invalidation
All user requests began hitting the database instead of cache
Slow unoptimized query on /api/orders consumed connections for 3+ seconds each
New requests queued waiting for connections
Response times escalated to 2+ seconds causing user-facing timeouts
